children.heading: .—Allotment of Space and Provision of Services Authorized
children.label: a .—Allotment of Space and Provision of Services Authorized

children.text: the WIC office provides or will provide services solely to members of the armed forces assigned to the installation, civilian employees of the Department of Defense employed at the installation, or dependents of such members or employees;
space is available on the installation;
operation of the WIC office will not hinder military mission requirements; and
the security situation at the installation permits the presence of a non-Federal entity on the installation.
The term “services” includes the provision of lighting, heating, cooling, and electricity.

The term “WIC office” means a local agency (as defined in subsection (b)(6) of section 17 of the Child Nutrition Act of 1966 ()) that participates in the special supplemental nutrition program for women, infants, and children under such section.42 U.S.C. 1786
